sections.paragraph: πῶλος, ου, ὁ [cp. Lat. pullus ‘the young of a horse or ass’] in NT only in recitals of Jesus’ entry into Jerusalem, colt, specifically, in light of immediate context, a young donkey Mt 21:2, 5, 7; J 12:15. In Mk 11:2, 4f, 7; Lk 19:30, 33, 35 π. can signify, given the absence of any specification, the foal of a horse.
